title: 2026 01 07 Rankett Meetup Prep Gdocs
html:
TL;DR

  • The Google-Docs version of the Rankett meetup prep brief written for the GEO & AI Visibility Meetup, January 2026 — parallel to the apple-notes GTM bible but in a more presentable doc format.
  • Codifies the now-canonical pitch: $998/seat wholesale → $3–6k/mo agency retail → $2–5k pure margin per client; "prompts are the new keywords."
  • 5+ active clients minimum + 60 day activation rule explicitly framed as "filter for action-takers, not tire-kickers" — same gate-keeping logic as Jan 5.
  • 100% ghost white label reiterated as architectural commitment — "not just cosmetic, it's strategic; their clients can't go direct because they don't know Rankett exists."
  • 50-question Q&A bank designed for live-meetup defense: pricing, billing model, AI visibility scope, non-guarantees, no-custom-builds, partner tiers, pooled data network effect.

What's new vs. the apple-notes version

This Google Doc is the clean version — apple-notes was the brain-dump. The structural differences:

  • The "Three-User Model" table (Rankett internal / Agency / End Client) is rendered as a real table here rather than inline prose.
  • Partner tiers are formalized: Tier 1 (1–4 clients, baseline email support), Tier 2 (5–9, faster response), Tier 3 (10+, priority + roadmap input).
  • The "Old Model vs New Model" comparison is sharpened — one avatar, one offer, one funnel, standardized fulfillment, compounding proof.
  • The 50 Q&A is expanded with technical-stack disclosure ("Next.js, React, TypeScript, Tailwind, Supabase, plus integrations to OpenAI, Claude, and Gemini APIs") — appears here for the first time.

Why this version exists

Yahir wrote two versions in the same week because the solo founder mode keeps generating the same content in different surfaces. The Google Doc is the version he could share with a co-presenter or attendee; the apple-notes version is the one he kept editing on his phone walking around Austin.

Key entities surfaced

rankett · ai visibility engine · ghost white label · 60 day activation rule · pooled data network effect · whitelabel strategy · ai search optimization · chatgpt · Claude · gemini · supabase
